reviews.review_msg: Sophie was my favourite professor this semester! She loves connecting with her students and was extremely forgiving with extensions during and after the TSSU strike. The books she chooses are excellent starters and she always finds a way to make her lectures engaging. Her TAs are also amazing (shoutout to Tianne)!

the professor was overall very kind, she explains all the assignments in the lectures super well and gives more than enough time for each assignment to be submitted. The course is overall pretty easy if you follow your weekly readings and participate in your tutorials.

I loved class with Dr. McCall. She was a caring and warm professor and encouraged participation. I loved the texts we read in class, the lectures were interesting and she brought in 3 guest speakers! She was accommodating during the strike and I would take another ENGL class with her. However, sometimes her docs/outlines were disorganized a bit.

Her lectures are a bit boring and she never responds to emails, but the books you read are very interesting. Marking is based of your TA and they mark pretty tough.  This class is not a class to improve your writing skills. Average for the written essay's were a C+.

Sophie is an amazing professor and genuinely cares a lot for her students and for the world. All of the novels we read are incredible and dealt with real historical events. The grading comes from the TAs and is pretty difficult considering this is a first year course. However, I would definitely recommend this class for first all year students.

I found this class to be very easy to manage with Dr. Mccall! Her lectures were interesting, and she was super understanding. Also people complaining about reading are silly because that's what anyone should expect from an english course? We read 5 main texts, so it was not bad at all. I had Maya as my TA and she was awesome! Loved this class!

Sophie was a good professor, her lectures were useful & helped with readings. The course had 2 heavy essays and she was lenient with the first essay deadline. Beware we are NEVER taught to write in this class. The TA's feedback on essays was minimal and unhelpful. The grading of this class is hard, especially given that we're first year students.
Super kind and intelligent prof. Played music at the beginning of every class. Engaging, guest speakers and overall interesting content.
Get ready to answer questions during lectures. Also DO NOT FORGET to turn in the FINAL ESSAY ASSIGNMENT DURING the LAST (maybe 2nd to last) LECTURE!

Pros:
- lots of reading
- a few opportunities to practice essay writing
- not many assignments

Cons:
- lots of reading
- niche topics
- nasally speaking voice
- lecture is a ramble, not as helpful as TA sessions. 
- McCall will create open a discussion for the students, then recycle the ideas for her next lecture.
Loved the lectures; grading comes from your TA's rather than the prof. Pretty easy class overall as long as you understand a couple of the text well

McCall's lectures were engaging, and included important insights on stories and essay feedback/information. Try to attend them all if you can because there are also participation activities that count towards your mark. Attending and speaking in tutorials is graded too so go to those! Readings were interesting and essays were simple.

Lectures and tutorials were chill; texts were also pretty interesting and enlightening focusing on a bunch of different cultural/topical issues. Dr. McCall always made sure people were comfortable when it came to some of the more heavier topics the texts introduced; also gave lots of prep and tips for the essays and final which helped a lot.

Super fun, enthusiastic professor. Often does activities in-lecture that count towards your participation, so make sure to attend them. Readings overall were not difficult; there was a wide range from graphic novels to poetry to traditional novels. Your grades are decided more by your TA so attend/participate in tutorials too!
prof was pretty funny during lectures, made the class very chill. The writing of couple of essays was fairly easy. The final was easy, questions given beforehand. would recommend.

Even though the course was online and the workload may be heavy, I found her prerecorded lectures informative and extremely useful in getting through the course; some of these reviews feel like they were written by people who honestly can't handle self-directed learning. Sophie is a pleasure to have for in-person lectures!

This class is just awful. I took it because it's a mandatory class for my program but it was probably in the top 2 worst classes I've taken at SFU. Really, DO NOT take it unless you have to. Readings are interesting but course is extremely uningaging. The marking scheme is unclear and the TA marked unnecessarily harsh with little feedback.

The class content is interesting in the sense of books and topics we are focusing on. However, for an online class, the syllabus was hard to follow and disorganized. Every week has a big chunk of material to go over, like reading a whole book or watching a 2hr movie. I would recommend it to someone that doesn't have a heavy course load.

My biggest gripe outside of what everyone else has said, is that the assignment instructions were not only vague but contradictory. Why not just make what you want of us clear instead of writing your own essay? I have had not direct interaction with her but after watching some lectures and reading her write-ups/assignments, I never want to. Awful

Her lectures are useless, she gives a lot of work and didn't think carefully about the dates. Some people would be expected to be a week ahead for their discussions or else they would have to read a whole book in a day. Her instructions are vague and she says um every other word during lectures. The lectures are pretty cringe overall. Bad prof

She only set up the course and recorded lectures but I would say that both aren't great. The course has a heavy workload and the lectures, besides vague, are unbearable because she writes on a sheet of glass in front of her the whole time (so edgy) and it makes awful nails on a chalkboard sounds. The TA is nice but like most TAs, a tough marker

I took this class asynchronously. Sophie had far more assignments than my other English classes. Every week had a module with readings and a few paragraphs introducing it. She occasionally had 10 minute lectures posted, but they did not connect readings. This class is better suited for in-person or synchronous, than offline. The readings were good.

I think most of what others said are true, her classes tends to slightly skewed towards the 'cultural/aboriginal studies'. Her reading list is good and sometimes she is not clear on what she wants. But she is a prof that genuinely wants you to do well and responsive to questions. Overall she is a great prof in her own way I guess.

the course content isn't difficult & she's nice but be prepared to read a lot... lectures were pretty much useless and you could get more from sparknotes or wikipedia, she seems to have a clear political agenda that she's pushing throughout the class and her interpretations feel like a gender studies course. no real depth or true literary analysis

Sophie McCall is an amazing lecturer. She really knows how to engage students and brings forward interesting discussion questions. Fair marker. Easy to talk to and always seems interested in helping students. Engl 357 had a great book list chosen by her.

She's a nice person but as a prof the class was disorganized. The lecture content was very superficial, never went in depth with analysis. Barely had to show up to class, just attend tutorials and you'll do fine. Presentation sign ups are on the first day, look into the books before hand for when you need to choose and defend one.

Enjoyed the class. Sophie is nice and reasonable. I didn't enjoy the group projects in tutorials - everyone gets the same grade and I'm just not a fan of group projects and presentations. It wasn't horrible but requires time outside of class to meet with group. Assigned small tasks in tutorials for the next week. Nice teacher but makes you work.

Sophie is a nice person, and goes easy with marking but is intimating sometimes when she doesn't agree with someone or something you're saying, which kind of seems like she is trying to intentionally make you look stupid. She has crazy eyes, but that doesn't bug me unless I look her right in the eye. Don't need to go to class pretty much ever.

Sophie's class sounded interesting but wasn't really very well put together. Readings were irrelevant and it felt like the class got less organized as we went on. She seemed to have a superficial grasp of some of the readings and preferred her own interpretations over opposing ones. OTOH, she is friendly, intelligent, and challenges her students.

There were 2 essays and one presentation with short story readings each week so altogether it's not a lot of work. The marking is fair and go to your TA to discuss your thesis before you start. Sophie is very nice prof that tries though her lectures are boring.

Lectures felt very long for 50 minutes. Says 'um' all the time. Midterm was worth 2.5% and asked ridiculous questions. Two essays that don't generalise to other academic writing: a close reading of a paragraph and a compare-contrast paper, which might eventually be useful, but not when everything else is research essays. Wouldn't take her again.

Midterm was worth 5% & was such a waste of time. Reading and essays were fair. Essays marked pretty hard though, get your thesis and argument approved by TA and you'll likely do better on the essay. Sophie was cute, lectures weren't that bad although she was just talking about what we read. I would take a class with her again.

Lectures are extremely boring and useless, she basically summarizes the stories you read. She cannot explain herself concisely so it's hard to understand what she's trying to say when it takes her 20 sentences to get it out. She seems helpful and kind, but it's always uncomfortable one on one. I wouldn't take a class with her again.

Lectures are extremely useless and boring. Over 30 short stories to read in the class, and her midterm required you to remember extremely minor, unimportant details. She mostly talks about her own opinions of the stories during lectures; she's kind in person but can be intimidating one-on-one. Not helpful when it comes to teaching.

Happy to never take a class with her again. Lecture is a painful hour of listening to her opinion on boring short stories instead of teaching things that will actually benefit us. Workload is fair as are essays. Talk to your TA about your thesis statement for the essay before handing it in to make sure you do well. Sum: try to get a different prof

Papers are marked pretty hard. Sophie was nice though, she was cute and had good ideas about the readings. Stay on top of the readings and participate in tutorials lots. Midterm was only worth 5% Final was worth more though but not too bad. The amount of work was fair. Lectures were a bit boring.

Easiest class I ever took at SFU. Sophie's literature choices are not always the most interesting. Readings are required, but posts lecture notes online so attendance is only required if you want participation credit for in-class activities. Essay questions posted ahead of time and all multiple choice are from the textbook website
Don't expect this to be a walk in the park. Sophie is really helpful and clear in what she wants for papers, but this isn't high school English anymore.

Sophie is a very kind prof with interesting ideas. She sheds new light on many pieces of literature. Sometimes it's difficult to understand what she's looking for, but once understood, assignments are easy (and scarce!). Good class.

Sophie encourages her students to do their best and recognizes hard work.  She is also really approachable and provides helpful feedback on assignments. Recommended for those who will appreciate a laid-back class environment that also stimulates one's mind.

I like Sophie, she's got a good attitude and her lectures are pretty good. Her written assignments were pretty good. However, her final exam was a bit harder than I expected. Some of her book choices weren't to my liking but that's a matter of taste. Overall she's good.

Definately a fun course and prof.  She makes the content interesting and really gets you to think about the material.  Make sure you keep up with the readings as the course moves quite quickly through the material.

Sophie is great. She communicates her material effectively as well as chooses material that is interesting and engaging. She marks fairly, is understanding and lenient with extensions and is so friendly and approachable. She really is one of the best Eng. profs @ SFU. One piece of advice though: stay away from passive voice!
Tests and assignments were extremely fair. Really prepared you for the exam. Good course to take for a first year student. Very approachable person.
She's good at leading tutorials. I like the books she chose.

Sophie is one of those professors whose young appearance and bubbly attitude obscures a brilliant mind.  Take her upper division courses with smaller class size.  Very bright, fun, enthusiastic.  A joy to go to class.  Beware: she has definite expectations about quality writing (and rightly so).

Sophie must have read all the comments below or something, because she doesn't say "umm" all the time, she's figured out the multimedia, and her classes were very interesting.  Her enthusiasm was infectious.  The only thing I would have liked would be more depth.

Lecures are exhausting. She rarely posts the notes before class & speaks VERY fast. I agree with the ummmm thing & watching her walk back and forth infront of the class made me busy. Reading were interesting those and so was the assignment. Just not the best prof I've had.

you can tell she enjoys what she is doing and it rubs off on you.  However, when it comes to the assignments (where the marks are) she is unclear.  examples of what she is looking for would be helpful.
No Comments

Sophie puts together a good English course.  Her outline is organized, she provides references, handouts, and other valuable material to encourage student research. Cares about her students doing well. Loves art, shows slides, field trip to art gallery.
Very enthusiastic, knows her material and is really involved with literary community. Great field trips and new ideas. For a required course it was a fantastic surprise.

she's not great AT ALL in a large classroom setting, but i had her for my tutorial leader, and she's great in a small room setting.  thinks of really neat, quirky activities to get the students into the reading material.  take her seminar courses!
Sophie is a great prof!
ummmmm...worse eng teacher ever...i don't even remember how many times i've fallen asleep in her class...so boring and useless
She needs to take public speaking class.  I stopped going to lecture (as did half the class) because I couldn't handle listening to her say UM any more.  I would never take her again.
organized, flexible, & with good ideas, but not a particularly good/experienced lecturer

So... UMMMMMMMM...  she says that about 200 times a lecture (not even kidding, people counted!) NOT a very good speaker, I avoid lecture just so I don't have to listen to her talk, the class is crap too.  nothing good to say about any of it.
This is a reallly good professor. She expains things very well and makes reading a better experience.
She's damn fine, but i cnat write an essay to save my life
